items.title: Tesla lithium refinery discharge contains toxic metals, drainage district demands halt
items.summary: Independent lab testing has found traces of hexavalent chromium - a known carcinogen - along with arsenic and elevated levels of lithium in wastewater discharged from Tesla's nearly $1 billion lithium refinery in Robstown, Texas. The...

items.title: First MacBook Neo deal makes Apple's budget laptop even more of a steal
items.summary: It's already a great deal, but shaving a few bucks of the MacBook Neo makes it even more attractive to laptop buyers. (via Cult of Mac - Your source for the latest Apple news, rumors, analysis, reviews, how-tos and deals. )

items.title: Apple executive in charge of Siri revamp has considered stepping back, report says
items.summary: Bloomberg reports that amid recent leadership changes at Apple, Mike Rockwell, who created the Vision Pro and now oversees efforts to revamp Siri, has considered leaving or reducing his role at the company. Here are the details.

items.title: Latest 'Star Wars' movie cut unnecessary costs by using Apple Vision Pro
items.summary: Director Jon Favreau says a specialized app let him better frame IMAX shots using a virtual theater environment in Apple Vision Pro . He cites it as one method to cut back on reshoots and reduce costs. Apple Vision Pro could become a...

items.title: Meta will record employees' keystrokes and use it to train its AI models
items.summary: Meta says that it has a new internal tool that is converting mouse movements and button clicks into data that can train its AI models.

items.title: This detail about Apple's CEO transition shows the company can still keep important secrets
items.summary: While it had long been expected that John Turnus would succeed Tim Cook as Apple's CEO, a key detail in the transition process shows that the company can still keep things under wraps when it counts. Here's why.

items.title: US wind just had a big comeback - and 2026 could be even bigger
items.summary: The US wind industry bounced back in a big way in 2025, installing 8.2 gigawatts (GW) of new capacity - up 49% from the year before - according to Wood Mackenzie's latest US Wind Energy Monitor report.
